task_completion.description: Construct triangle ABC with vertices A(1, 1), B(7, 2), and C(4, 6)
task_completion.visual_evidence: A triangle is visible in the GeoGebra graphics view. The Algebra view or grid should confirm the vertices are at (1, 1), (7, 2), and (4, 6).

task_completion.description: Construct the Centroid (G) of the triangle
task_completion.visual_evidence: A point (often labeled G) is visible at coordinates (4.0, 3.0). The Algebra view should show it as a dependent point or intersection (e.g., of medians).

task_completion.description: Construct the Circumcenter (O) of the triangle
task_completion.visual_evidence: A point (often labeled O) is visible near coordinates (3.8, 2.7). The Algebra view should show it as a dependent point or intersection (e.g., of perpendicular bisectors).

task_completion.description: Construct the Orthocenter (H) of the triangle
task_completion.visual_evidence: A point (often labeled H) is visible near coordinates (4.4, 3.6). The Algebra view should show it as a dependent point or intersection (e.g., of altitudes).

task_completion.description: Construct the Euler line connecting the Centroid, Circumcenter, and Orthocenter
task_completion.visual_evidence: A straight line is drawn that passes exactly through the three constructed center points (G, O, and H).

task_completion.description: Include a text annotation identifying the centers or describing the line
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text box is visible in the GeoGebra graphics view containing descriptive text about the Euler line or identifying the specific centers.
The GeoGebra window title changes to 'euler_line.ggb' or a save dialog/file manager confirms the file was saved in the '~/Documents/GeoGebra/projects/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the GeoGebra GUI to construct the geometry
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the GeoGebra application open with construction steps progressing over time, rather than terminal commands generating the file.

integrity.description: The agent used geometric tools or GeoGebra commands to find the centers, rather than hardcoding free points
integrity.visual_evidence: The GeoGebra Algebra view should show the centers as dependent objects (e.g., intersections of lines, or results of commands like Centroid[poly1]) rather than manually entered free points at the exact decimal coordinates.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a text editor to manually write the .ggb (ZIP/XML) file
integrity.visual_evidence: No text editors (like nano, vim, gedit) are seen open editing raw XML or GeoGebra file contents.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The triangle vertices are A(1, 1), B(7, 2), and C(4, 6). The Centroid (G) is exactly at (4.0, 3.0). The Circumcenter (O) is approximately at (3.796, 2.722). The Orthocenter (H) is approximately at (4.407, 3.556). The Euler line must pass through these three collinear points. The expected save path is /home/ga/Documents/GeoGebra/projects/euler_line.ggb.
